summary: The author shares her recent diagnosis of Graves' Disease, an autoimmune disorder causing thyroid hyperactivity, anxiety, and compulsive exercise. She explains the condition is treatable with medication (Methimazole) or potentially radioactive iodine therapy. The diagnosis actually brought relief by explaining her deep anxiety, sleep troubles, and rapidly changing eyesight. She notes that quitting Twitter has helped her anxiety, and she's channeling her hyperactive energy into training for races. The post ends with a plug for her band's upcoming gig.
key_points: Graves' Disease causes thyroid hyperactivity leading to anxiety, sleep problems, and compulsive exercise - but it's treatable
Autoimmune diseases cluster together; she already had autoimmune gastritis before this diagnosis
The diagnosis provided relief by explaining mysterious symptoms like deep anxiety and rapidly changing eyesight
Quitting social media has been beneficial for managing anxiety
She's channeling the hyperactive energy into training for a 5K and a sprint triathlon
money_quotes: It's an autoimmune disease, and it's treatable, so please don't worry.
Once you've gotten one autoimmune disease you're more likely to get another. They cluster.
Finding out I have Graves' Disease is actually kind of good. It explains the deep anxiety I was feeling and the trouble I was having sleeping.
I'm not sure if that's because the meds are working or because I'm just acclimating to Trump.
Or it might just be because I quit Twitter, which was bringing me down consistently. Having no social media habits is good for anxiety problems!
I've always said that people who get really into exercise are the most boring people in the world because all they ever want to talk about is exercise.
I've really become insufferable. Please tell me to shut up when you next hear me drone on.
key_insight: A medical diagnosis can be strangely liberating when it finally explains inexplicable feelings of anxiety and unrest.
